title: Bagnath Temple
tagline: Bagnath Temple — spiritual town of Uttarakhand

overview.short: Bagnath Temple (Kumaoni: बागनाथ थान) is an ancient shrine dedicated to Shiva, situated in the Bageshwar city at the confluence of Sarayu and Gomati rivers.
overview.description: Bagnath Temple (Kumaoni: बागनाथ थान) is an ancient shrine dedicated to Shiva, situated in the Bageshwar city at the confluence of Sarayu and Gomati rivers. Bagnath Temple is festooned with bells of all sizes and features impressive carvings. It is the most famous Temple in Bageshwar District. It is flooded with devotees on the occasion of Shivratri.

topPlaces.name: Mankot
topPlaces.description: Mankot is a Village situated in Bageshwar district in the State of Uttarakhand, India. It is located at a distance of 13.5 kilometres (8.4 mi) from Bageshwar on the National Highway 309A. Mankot is a medium-sized village with total 118 families residing.

topPlaces.name: Kanda, Uttarakhand
topPlaces.description: Kanda is a small historic town and tehsil in Bageshwar district, in the state of Uttarakhand, India.

topPlaces.name: Bageshwar
topPlaces.description: Bageshwar (Kumaoni: Bāgshyār) is a town and a municipal board in Bageshwar district in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is located at a distance of 470 km from the National Capital New Delhi and 332 km from the State Capital Dehradun.

topPlaces.name: Golu Devata
topPlaces.description: Golu Devata (Kumaoni: गोलज्यू) is a deity of the Kumaoni community of India. Also known as the god of justice (Hindi: न्याय के देवता).

topPlaces.name: Kapkot
topPlaces.description: Kapkot or Kapkote is a town in Bageshwar district, Uttarakhand, India. It houses the headquarters of Kapkot Tehsil, the largest administrative subdivision of Bageshwar district.

topPlaces.name: Sani Udiyar
topPlaces.description: Sani Udiyar is a village situated in Kanda Tehsil in Bageshwar district in the State of Uttarakhand, India. It is located at a distance of 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Kanda and 36 kilometres (22 mi) from Bageshwar.

topPlaces.name: Vijaypur, Uttarakhand
topPlaces.description: Vijaypur, officially known as Bijaypur is a hill station and village situated in Bageshwar district in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is located at a distance of 30 km from Bageshwar and 5 km from Kanda; amidst dense Pine Forests on the Bageshwar-Chaukori Highway.

topPlaces.name: Khantoli
topPlaces.description: Khantoli is a small village located at an elevation of 5000 ft in Bageshwar district of Uttrakhand. The village is located in Himalayan rage of Uttrakhand.

items.note: Mankot is a Village situated in Bageshwar district in the State of Uttarakhand, India. It is located at a distance of 13.5 kilometres (8.4 mi) from Bageshwar on the National Highway 309A. Mankot is a medium-sized village with total 118 families residing.
Kanda is a small historic town and tehsil in Bageshwar district, in the state of Uttarakhand, India.

Bageshwar (Kumaoni: Bāgshyār) is a town and a municipal board in Bageshwar district in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is located at a distance of 470 km from the National Capital New Delhi and 332 km from the State Capital Dehradun.
Golu Devata (Kumaoni: गोलज्यू) is a deity of the Kumaoni community of India. Also known as the god of justice (Hindi: न्याय के देवता).
Kapkot or Kapkote is a town in Bageshwar district, Uttarakhand, India. It houses the headquarters of Kapkot Tehsil, the largest administrative subdivision of Bageshwar district.

Sani Udiyar is a village situated in Kanda Tehsil in Bageshwar district in the State of Uttarakhand, India. It is located at a distance of 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Kanda and 36 kilometres (22 mi) from Bageshwar.

Vijaypur, officially known as Bijaypur is a hill station and village situated in Bageshwar district in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is located at a distance of 30 km from Bageshwar and 5 km from Kanda; amidst dense Pine Forests on the Bageshwar-Chaukori Highway.
Khantoli is a small village located at an elevation of 5000 ft in Bageshwar district of Uttrakhand. The village is located in Himalayan rage of Uttrakhand.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bagnath Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bagnath Temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bagnath Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pantnagar Airport (95 km) and the nearest railway station is Kathgodam (Nainital railhead) (69 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bagnath Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bagnath Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bagnath Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bagnath Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bagnath Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mankot, Kanda, Uttarakhand, Bageshwar, Golu Devata, Kapkot.
